mnemonics: Make & Remove Directories: Like a librarian Making new shelves (mkdir) and Removing empty ones (rmdir).
keyPoints: mkdir() creates a new, empty directory with a specified pathname and permission mode.
rmdir() removes an existing directory, but only if it is empty (contains no files or subdirectories).
Both functions return 0 on success and -1 on failure, with the error code stored in the global variable errno.
commonMistakes: Attempting to use rmdir() on a non-empty directory, which will fail with the ENOTEMPTY error.
Forgetting to set the proper file permissions (mode) when creating a directory with mkdir(), often leading to access issues.
